$comment_spec: Anthropic MCPB (formerly DXT) manifest, spec v0.3 — https://github.com/anthropics/dxt/blob/main/MANIFEST.md
$comment_remote: MCPB v0.3 does not define a native 'remote' server type for hosted Streamable HTTP MCP servers. Until it does, this bundle ships a tiny stdio<->HTTP shim via `mcp-remote` (https://www.npmjs.com/package/mcp-remote), which handles the MCP OAuth 2.0 + DCR handshake against api.loovie.app/v1/mcp and exposes the result as a local stdio MCP server that Claude Desktop can spawn.
name: loovie-mcp
description: Create AI characters, generate images and videos, and edit Loovie projects from Claude Desktop. Connects to the hosted Loovie MCP server (OAuth, Streamable HTTP).
long_description: Loovie is an AI video creation platform. This extension exposes 100+ tools and 20+ resources covering characters, image and video generation (text-to-image, image-to-video, first/last frame, swap, motion control, AI VFX), timeline editing, library assets, exports, and credit management. Every spend tool follows an estimate / approval / execute flow so Claude Desktop always confirms credit cost before any generation runs. On first use a browser window opens for OAuth sign-in; the token is then cached locally by the mcp-remote bridge.
$comment_icon: Icon path is relative to the bundle root. Drop a 512x512 PNG at loovie-mcp/icon.png to enable; until then this field is omitted so `mcpb pack` doesn't fail validation.
